description: Deterministic lexicon precision/recall self-gate for the operator-attested-evidence-kind advisory DET lint (extends p6_ac_quality). NO LLM anywhere — the lint and this eval are pure regex over AC text.

corpus.provenance: AC checklist items of the 556 E1 outcome-corpus tickets (runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl) that resolve in the store, extracted from ticket descriptions; tag stripped before flagging to simulate the historical untagged plan.

recall.note: recall vs the 31 human-tagged ground-truth positives (imperfect: untagged items may be unlabeled positives). A precision-first advisory lint deliberately trades recall for precision.

precision.method: full census of the flagged set (>= the required 50-item sample), frozen per-item TP/FP adjudication

adjudications.ac_text: - [ ] Command lives in PROJECT tooling (repo CLI/script), NOT `rebar` core; may `import rebar` only for ticket ops (e.g. close the ticket on merge). No Gerrit-specific landing logic in `src/rebar`. **Gerrit REST access reuses this repo's proven STDLIB-ONLY Gerrit-client approach** — the `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` pattern (`urllib` + HTTP Basic auth + XSSI stripping, *"deliberately NO new httpx dependency"*): NOT a new third-party client (not `pygerrit2`) and NOT a from-scratch reimplementation. Packaged as a **thin stdlib helper in `infra/autolander`** (mirrors the proven `urllib`+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern; no new dependency; honors the project-tooling/core boundary; unification behind the platform provider-seam is deferred to idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`). Auth is the **agent's OWN ambient Gerrit HTTP credential** read from the standard place (`.netrc` / the git credential helper the agent already uses to push to Gerrit) and passed as a stdlib `urllib` Basic-auth header — no separate credential-injection mechanism to invent.
adjudications.label: FP
adjudications.reason: code-structure/placement AC (where the helper lives, stdlib pattern) — verifiable in-repo

adjudications.label: TP

adjudications.ac_text: - [ ] **S1 — Gate + ACL config** (`infra/gerrit/project.config`, `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`, declarative): LLM-Review `copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` (push accepted by the running 3.14.1); Verified copyCondition UNCHANGED (re-run posture) with the rationale recorded; record the current effective inherited submit type (Gerrit REST/ssh query, noted on the ticket) THEN pin `[submit] action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true` in a quiet window; ACLs: `Create Reference`/`Delete Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*` + `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` and `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*`, ALL bound to the named `feature-branch-drivers` group — including extending `setup-project.sh`'s managed-groups `want` dict (~line 57, currently only Administrators + Service Users) so the group is created/resolved declaratively; ADR amending ADR-0013 (+ 1fa8's Verified ADR) recording the merge-change gate, label-divergence rationale, and the group membership policy; `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` gate/ACL sections updated. Verified live on the running Gerrit.
adjudications.label: TP

adjudications.ac_text: - [ ] **S5 — Live multi-agent E2E validation** on the running Gerrit (scripted alongside `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`; every scenario's change URLs + votes recorded on this ticket): (1) **parallel siblings** — two agents in separate worktrees push independent story changes to `feature/e2e-<date>`; both earn LLM-Review + Verified and submit WITHOUT either rebasing (second submit succeeds via MERGE_IF_NECESSARY with no manual rebase; this also live-proves webhook + g2p branch-agnostic dispatch); (2) **stacking** — a third agent pulls the branch (both stories in history) and pushes a change on top; the reviewed diff covers ONLY its delta; (3) **merge-back** — `--no-ff` merge change to main: bot reviews the auto-merge delta only (assert NOT the whole-feature diff), CI green on the merge tree, both votes present, submit lands atomically, replication advances GitHub main with all commits, branch deleted; (4) **vote-carry on re-merge** — land an unrelated change on main mid-flight, push a re-merge (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE): LLM-Review +1 CARRIES, Verified RE-RUNS; then change the feature tip: BOTH wiped (REWORK); (5) **textual conflict** — overlapping feature-vs-main edits resolved in the merge commit; bot reviews the non-empty auto-merge delta; (6) **semantic conflict** — textually-clean but semantically broken combination (e.g. rename on main + new call-site on feature): auto-merge diff empty, LLM-Review passes, CI red → Verified=-1 BLOCKS submit (proves two-vote complementarity); (7) **races + negatives** — concurrent submits into the branch behave (MERGE_IF_NECESSARY or clear conflict signal); a merge push WITHOUT pushMerge is refused; branch creation outside `feature-branch-drivers` is refused; the reconciler backfills a dropped webhook for a feature-branch change (including a merge change, exercising the transparent merge detection).
adjudications.label: TP

adjudications.label: TP

adjudications.label: FP
adjudications.reason: code-structure AC (helper reuses stdlib pattern) — verifiable in-repo; 'provisioned' is incidental
